Container.Name: patchpulse
Container.Overview:
See what changed before you update a container. Release notes + CVE context for your Docker stack.
PRE-RELEASE BETA. Actively testing; report issues on GitHub.
What it does: - Discovers your Docker containers via the socket - Polls Docker Hub / GHCR / Quay for new versions (6h default) - Fetches changelog (GitHub Releases -> CHANGELOG.md -> commit log) - Scans the currently-running image for CVEs via bundled grype - Shows you the decision surface: changelog + CVE calls + "Update now" - Notifies via Apprise (ntfy, Gotify, Discord, Telegram, 110+ channels)
Diun tells you a new version exists. What's Up Docker adds scheduling. Neither shows you the changelog or flags CVEs in the version you're running. PatchPulse does.
Safety: - "Update now" is disabled for containers started with 'docker run' (only works when the container's compose-file path is configured) - Requires you to re-type the service name as a confirmation token - Docker socket mounted read-only by default (no update action) - Enable writes only by editing the socket mount + enable_update_action
Setup: 1. Edit /etc/patchpulse/config.yml 2. Generate auth hash: read -rs PW; echo -n "$PW" | docker exec -i patchpulse /usr/local/bin/patchpulse hashpw 3. (Recommended) Add a GitHub PAT (public_repo read) for 5000 req/hr
Container.DonateText: If you like this, please consider a donation!
Container.Description: Release notes + CVE context for your Docker container updates. See what changed before you update, and whether the version you're running has unfixed vulnerabilities. PRE-RELEASE BETA.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: SQLite database of container history + CVE cache

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Docker socket. Read-only (default) = notify only. Switch to rw if you enable the Update action in config.yml.

Config.Name: Compose stacks directory
Config.Description: Root directory containing compose.yml files PatchPulse should be able to read. Duplicate if your stacks live in multiple roots.
